class.name: GameInputDevice
class.brief_description: Weak handle to a single GameInput device.
class.description: [GameInputDevice] is a GDScript-facing wrapper around an [code]IGameInputDevice[/code]. It stores only a session-local monotonic device id (a weak handle), so it is cheap to keep around in user code. All instance methods resolve through the [GameInput] singleton; if the device has been disconnected since the wrapper was handed out, every method returns a safe default ([code]false[/code], an empty [String], [code]-1.0[/code], an empty [Dictionary]) instead of crashing. Device ids are never recycled within a process, so a stored id remains a stable identity for as long as your game runs. Wrappers are obtained from [method GameInput.get_devices], [method GameInput.get_primary_device], or the [signal GameInput.device_connected] signal. The [enum Button], [enum Axis], and [enum Source] enums describe the input vocabulary used by [GameInputReading] and [GameInputBinding]. [enum Source] is a single combined namespace covering buttons (values 0–13) and axes (values 100–105) so editor dropdowns can show one unified list.

method.name: get_device_id
method.description: Returns the session-local monotonic id assigned when this device first connected. The id is never recycled and is the same value reported by [signal GameInput.device_disconnected]. Useful as a stable key in player-to-device dictionaries.

method.name: get_display_name
method.description: Returns the human-readable device name reported by GameInput (e.g. "Xbox Wireless Controller"). Falls back to a synthesized [code]"GameInput Device VVVV:PPPP"[/code] string built from vendor and product ids when no display name is available, or an empty [String] if the device has been disconnected.

method.name: get_kind_mask
method.description: Returns the device's supported-input mask as a bitfield of [GameInput] [enum GameInput.DeviceKind] flags. A single physical device may report multiple kinds (e.g. a controller that also emulates a keyboard). Returns [constant GameInput.DEVICE_UNKNOWN] when the device has been disconnected.

method.name: is_connected
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the device is still in the [GameInput] cache. Wrappers stay alive after disconnect, so user code holding a reference can safely call this to gate further work.

method.name: supports_vibration
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the device reports any rumble motors. Use this before calling [method GameInput.set_vibration] to avoid wasted calls on devices that cannot rumble.

method.name: supports_haptics
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the device reports one or more haptic-feedback motors (distinct from rumble). The current addon does not yet send haptic patterns; this is exposed so games can detect capability ahead of a future API.

method.name: get_battery_level
method.description: Returns the battery level as a value in [code][0.0, 1.0][/code], or [code]-1.0[/code] when the battery state is unknown, the device is wired/has no battery, or the device is disconnected. Use [code]< 0[/code] as the "no battery info" sentinel rather than treating low values as low battery.

method.name: get_device_info
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] describing the device. Keys include [code]name[/code] ([String]), [code]vendor_id[/code] ([int]), [code]product_id[/code] ([int]), [code]revision[/code] ([int]), [code]interface_number[/code] ([int]), [code]device_family[/code] ([int]), [code]supported_input_kinds[/code] ([int], the raw [code]GameInputKind[/code] mask), [code]supported_input_mask[/code] ([int], the [enum GameInput.DeviceKind] mask), [code]supports_vibration[/code] ([bool]), [code]controller_axis_count[/code], [code]controller_button_count[/code], [code]force_feedback_motor_count[/code], and [code]haptic_feedback_motor_count[/code]. Returns an empty [Dictionary] when the device is disconnected.

method.name: button_to_source
method.description: Translates a [enum Button] flag value (a single bit such as [constant BUTTON_A]) into the matching [enum Source] value used by [member GameInputBinding.source]. Returns [constant SRC_BTN_A] for unrecognized inputs as a safe default.

method.name: axis_to_source
method.description: Translates an [enum Axis] index value (such as [constant AXIS_LEFT_X]) into the matching [enum Source] value used by [member GameInputBinding.source]. Returns [constant SRC_AXIS_LEFT_X] for unrecognized inputs as a safe default.

constants.constant: Sentinel "no button" value. Not a valid argument to [method GameInputReading.is_button_down]; the reading methods will simply return [code]false[/code].
Bit flag for the gamepad Menu button (Start on Xbox controllers).
Bit flag for the gamepad View button (Back/Select on Xbox controllers).
Bit flag for the A face button.
Bit flag for the B face button.
Bit flag for the X face button.
Bit flag for the Y face button.
Bit flag for the D-pad up direction.
Bit flag for the D-pad down direction.
Bit flag for the D-pad left direction.
Bit flag for the D-pad right direction.
Bit flag for the left shoulder bumper (LB).
Bit flag for the right shoulder bumper (RB).
Bit flag for the left thumbstick click (L3).
Bit flag for the right thumbstick click (R3).
Index of the left thumbstick X axis. Returned values are in [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code].
Index of the left thumbstick Y axis. [method GameInputReading.get_axis] returns this with the sign flipped to match Godot's convention (down is positive).
Index of the right thumbstick X axis. Returned values are in [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code].
Index of the right thumbstick Y axis. Sign-flipped to match Godot's convention.
Index of the left analog trigger. Returned values are in [code][0.0, 1.0][/code].
Index of the right analog trigger. Returned values are in [code][0.0, 1.0][/code].
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the Menu button.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the View button.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the A face button. This is the default [member GameInputBinding.source].
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the B face button.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the X face button.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the Y face button.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the D-pad up direction.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the D-pad down direction.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the D-pad left direction.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the D-pad right direction.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the left shoulder bumper.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the right shoulder bumper.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the left thumbstick click.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the right thumbstick click.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the left thumbstick X axis. Bindings using axis sources should set [member GameInputBinding.is_axis] to [code]true[/code].
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the left thumbstick Y axis.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the right thumbstick X axis.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the right thumbstick Y axis.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the left analog trigger.
[member GameInputBinding.source] value for the right analog trigger.
